supporting_material_rows.detail: the historical theory/operator/served split must stay green/suppressed in the old posture before it can be rebound to the canonical route.
the historical closeout summary must keep served blockers and non-implications explicit so the rebased split preserves the old public boundary.
the bridge contract must stay green so the rebased split binds to one canonical machine identity instead of a mixed historical reconstruction.
the canonical-route universal-substrate gate must stay green before the rebased verdict split can publish theory/operator truth on the canonical route.
the portability/minimality matrix must stay green so theory/operator truth remains bound to the declared CPU machine matrix and explicit route-carrier classification.
the served conformance envelope must stay fail-closed so the rebased split can keep theory/operator truth separate from served/public universality.
the plugin/turing audit remains observational context here and explains why the rebased verdict split must not over-read future plugin capability.

verdict_rows.allowed_statement: Psionic/Tassadar can honestly say that the post-`TAS-186` canonical route on machine_identity_id=`tassadar.post_article_universality_bridge.machine_identity.v1` carries the declared `TCM.v1` universal substrate plus explicit universal-machine witness constructions under the declared checkpoint-and-resume semantics.
verdict_rows.detail: theory-green means there is now one declared universal substrate plus explicit witness encodings and a green minimal universal-substrate gate; it does not by itself imply operator or served widening Rebased theory truth is now bound to canonical_route_id=`tassadar.article_route.direct_hull_cache_runtime.v1` and canonical_route_descriptor_digest=`00af062cccc3d1b2e42ee8e092b9ef8e1539dd3f2dfc379cff7d1cd4a008b032`.

verdict_rows.allowed_statement: Psionic/Tassadar can honestly say that operators have one bounded universality-capable lane on the post-`TAS-186` canonical route under named session-process, spill/tape, and process-object envelopes with exact checkpoint-and-replay evidence.
verdict_rows.detail: operator-green means the universal construction survives only inside explicit resumable process envelopes with persisted state objects, spill/tape extension, and typed refusal on broader external loops or ambient effects Rebased operator truth is now frozen on machine_identity_id=`tassadar.post_article_universality_bridge.machine_identity.v1` with canonical_route_id=`tassadar.article_route.direct_hull_cache_runtime.v1`.

verdict_rows.allowed_statement: Psionic/Tassadar does not yet expose a served/public universality lane on the post-`TAS-186` canonical route; served posture remains the narrower `tassadar.internal_compute.article_closeout.v1` lane inside the declared conformance envelope.
verdict_rows.detail: served-green remains suppressed because the current served internal-compute lane is still article-closeout, not a published universality profile, and the authority-bearing served closure still lives outside standalone psionic The rebased served row remains suppressed because the served conformance envelope is fail-closed and served/public universality is still out of scope.

validation_rows.detail: helper substitution remains quarantined, so the rebased verdict split cannot inherit canonical-route truth through hidden host execution.
route drift remains rejected, so the rebased verdict split stays attached to one canonical route id and one descriptor digest.
continuation abuse remains quarantined, so operator truth still depends on explicit checkpoint-and-resume semantics rather than hidden control transfer.
semantic drift remains blocked, so the rebased verdict split does not treat portability or publication as permission to change the compute semantics.
historical universality artifacts remain preserved without rewrite, so the rebased split is explicit rebinding rather than narrative replacement.
the rebased verdict split stays bound to the declared canonical machine identity and machine matrix rather than inheriting truth from an unspecified host/runtime mix.
the served conformance envelope stays bound and fail-closed, so served posture remains the narrower article-closeout lane instead of silently widening into served universality.
the rebased theory/operator split is now allowed, but plugin capability, served/public universality, and arbitrary software capability remain explicitly out of scope.

claim_boundary: this report reissues the theory/operator/served universality verdict split on the post-`TAS-186` canonical machine identity. It binds the historical verdict split to the canonical owned route, the canonical weight artifact, the continuation contract, the portability/minimality matrix, and the served conformance envelope. It allows the rebased theory/operator claim only for the canonical route and still does not imply weighted plugin control, served/public universality, or arbitrary software capability.
summary: Post-article rebased universality verdict split keeps supporting_materials=7/7, validation_rows=8/8, verdict_split_status=TheoryGreenOperatorGreenServedSuppressed, rebase_claim_allowed=true, and served_green=false.
